112.name: Commuter S
112.description: Commuter S strips away everything unnecessary. What remains is the fastest, most reliable way to move through the city.

140.name: Volt E1
140.description: The Volt E1 redefines what an e-bike can be. With a 750Wh battery and intelligent motor management, range anxiety is a thing of the past.

141.name: Terra
141.description: Terra goes where other bikes won't. Titanium frame, wide tyre clearance, and SRAM AXS wireless shifting for when the road runs out.

143.name: Aero One
143.description: The Aero One is our flagship road racing machine. Every gram is justified, every curve optimised in the wind tunnel.

144.name: Summit X9
144.description: The Summit X9 is a pure mountain bike built for aggressive trail riding. Full carbon frame, 150mm travel front and rear, and geometry that begs you to push harder into every corner.

145.name: Apex SL
145.description: The Apex SL is built for riders who refuse to compromise. An ultralight carbon frame, a Shimano Ultegra Di2 groupset, and a geometry tuned for long-distance speed. This is a road bike that rewards commitment.

146.name: Ridgeline Pro
146.description: Ridgeline Pro is a gravel bike with no fear of distance. Chromoly steel frame, mounts for everything, and a ride quality that softens even the roughest tracks. Pack light. Go far.

147.name: Trail E2
147.description: The Trail E2 puts electric power where it belongs — on the mountain. With 140mm of travel and a Shimano EP801 motor, it amplifies your ability without dulling the ride. Every trail becomes more achievable, every climb more enjoyable.

148.name: Metro E2
148.description: Metro E2 is the urban e-bike for riders who take their commute seriously. A Bosch Active Line Plus motor handles the hills, a 500Wh battery handles the distance, and a step-through frame handles everything in between.

149.name: Enduro X
149.description: Enduro X is engineered for riders who judge a ride by the descent. Full carbon frame, 170mm of travel, and a RockShox Lyrik fork that devours whatever the mountain throws at it. Pedal up. Attack down.

150.name: Sprint R1
150.description: The Sprint R1 is a no-compromise criterium racer. Stiff, aggressive, and fast. An aluminium frame with race geometry puts you exactly where you need to be — on the limit, every lap.

151.name: Horizon Tour
151.description: Horizon Tour is the endurance road bike for riders who measure routes in days. A relaxed geometry, carbon fork, and wide tyre clearance make it equally at home on a century ride or a multi-day tour.

152.name: Velox Pro
152.description: Velox Pro is a carbon road bike built for serious club riders and Gran Fondo athletes. The layup prioritises vertical compliance without sacrificing lateral stiffness — so you push harder, for longer.

153.name: Pavé Classic
153.description: The Pavé Classic takes inspiration from the cobbled monuments. Relaxed head tube, flared drops, and a steel fork that absorbs what the road throws at it. Built to last. Built to endure.

154.name: Canyon
154.description: Canyon is a lightweight gravel bike that doesn't take shortcuts. A full carbon frame, 1x drivetrain, and 42c tyres give you the confidence to explore deeper. Built for adventure riders who like to move fast.

155.name: Stray
155.description: Stray is the bikepacking gravel bike you've been waiting for. Mounts for frame bags, fork cages, and a rear rack. Geometry that stays stable under load. Take everything you need and go.

156.name: Gravel X3
156.description: Gravel X3 is the race-ready choice. An aerodynamic carbon layup, Zipp 303 wheels, and an SRAM Force AXS drivetrain. When the gravel race clock is running, this is what you want under you.

157.name: Basalt
157.description: Basalt is built for the rider who doesn't check the weather forecast. A rust-resistant stainless steel frame, mechanical disc brakes, and clearance for 50c tyres. Reliable. Durable. Ready.

158.name: Flint
158.description: Flint proves you don't need to spend a fortune to explore. An aluminium frame, 1x drivetrain, and hydraulic disc brakes give you everything that matters and nothing that doesn't. A gravel bike for the rest of us.

159.name: Hardtail HT1
159.description: Hardtail HT1 is a no-nonsense trail hardtail that rewards good technique. A lightweight aluminium frame, 120mm fork, and SRAM SX Eagle drivetrain. The bike that makes you a better rider.

160.name: Crest 130
160.description: Crest 130 is a trail bike designed around one thing: flow. 130mm of travel, progressive geometry, and a Shimano Deore drivetrain that lets you focus on the trail, not the gear.

161.name: Full Send
161.description: Full Send is the bike for riders who live for the descent. 160mm front and rear, a coil shock, and a mullet setup that turns steep lines into something you can actually enjoy. Commit. Send it.

162.name: Bolt E2
162.description: Bolt E2 is the next generation of the Bolt eMTB line. A more powerful motor, a longer-range battery, and updated geometry for confident descending. The mountain got smaller.

163.name: Spark E1
163.description: Spark E1 is a lightweight XC e-bike for riders who want to go higher, faster. A nimble 120mm carbon frame, a Fazua Ride 60 motor, and a full Shimano XTR spec. When the trail points up, you smile.

164.name: Ridge E3
164.description: Ridge E3 is the accessible all-mountain e-bike. A mid-range spec with no weak points. Bosch motor, 625Wh battery, and a geometry that handles technical terrain without demanding expert-level skill.

165.name: City Glide
165.description: City Glide is the urban e-bike that makes every commute feel like a pleasure. A Shimano STEPS motor, automatic 5-speed gearbox, and puncture-resistant tyres. Just get on and go.

166.name: Urban X Cargo
166.description: Urban X Cargo is the e-bike that replaces your car. A reinforced rear rack rated to 30kg, a powerful 85Nm motor, and hydraulic brakes give you everything you need to ditch the school run.

167.name: Fold E1
167.description: Fold E1 is a folding e-bike that refuses to compromise on ride quality. A 20" wheeled aluminium frame folds in under ten seconds, and a 250W hub motor gets you there without breaking a sweat.

168.name: Avenue S
168.description: Avenue S is an urban e-bike designed to look as good as it rides. A clean aluminium frame hides the cabling, the battery sits flush in the downtube, and the Bosch Smart System keeps everything connected.

169.name: Crossover CX1
169.description: Crossover CX1 bridges urban commuting and light trail riding. Wider tyres, a suspension fork, and a mid-drive motor handle both gravel paths and city streets without asking you to choose.

170.name: Swift
170.description: Swift strips urban cycling back to its essence. No suspension, no excess weight, no excuses. A rigid aluminium frame, flat bars, and a Mahle X35+ rear hub motor make it the fastest point-to-point in the city.

171.name: Peak R7
171.description: Peak R7 is a high-clearance road bike built for riders who refuse to be limited by tarmac. Drop bars, 35c clearance, and a carbon fork give you speed on the road and confidence when it gets rough.

172.name: Volante
172.description: Volante is our most elegant road bike. A hand-laid carbon frame, a full Campagnolo Chorus groupset, and a geometry tuned for the long alpine climbs. Beauty with a purpose.

173.name: Paceline E1
173.description: Paceline E1 is an electric road bike for riders who still want to work for it. A discreet Mahle ebikemotion X35 system adds just enough assistance to keep you in the break — without giving the game away.

174.name: Dust
174.description: Dust is the gravel bike for riders who like to move light and move fast. An aggressive carbon layup, 45c clearance, and a SRAM Rival AXS drivetrain. When the route calls for improvisation, Dust answers.

175.name: Mule
175.description: Mule earns its name. A chromoly steel frame built for heavy loads, four-season reliability, and distances that would stop lesser bikes. Racks, fenders, dynamo lighting — everything included, nothing optional.

176.name: Veer
176.description: Veer is the convertible in the HyBikes range. Swap the tyres and it becomes a different bike — fast 700x32c slicks for the road, chunky 650b x 47c blocks for the tracks. One frame. Infinite routes.

177.name: Loam
177.description: Loam is a steel hardtail built for riders who love getting their tyres dirty. A geometry that rewards commitment, a RockShox Judy fork, and a Shimano Deore groupset. Honest riding at an honest price.

178.name: Apex eMTB
178.description: Apex eMTB is the flagship electric mountain bike. Full carbon frame, 160mm travel, TQ HPR50 motor, and a spec sheet that leaves nothing to want. When only the best will do.

179.name: Switchback 130
179.description: Switchback 130 is a playful trail full-suspension built for tight, technical singletrack. 130mm front and rear, a low standover, and a short chainstay that makes the bike feel like an extension of your body.

180.name: Night Rider
180.description: Night Rider is the e-bike built for year-round commuters. A 90-lux integrated front light, reflective tyre sidewalls, and a Bosch motor with anti-lock braking. The weather can do what it wants.

181.name: Campus E1
181.description: Campus E1 is the affordable entry point into electric urban cycling. A reliable hub motor, a seven-speed drivetrain, and a sturdy aluminium frame. Everything you need, at a price that makes sense.

182.name: Ember E
182.description: Ember E is an electric gravel bike that extends your range without shrinking your ambition. A discreet Fazua Ride 60 motor, 430Wh battery, and an aluminium frame with full gravel geometry. Go further. Come back smiling.

183.name: Slate
183.description: Slate is the minimalist gravel bike. A matte carbon frame, a single chainring, and nothing on the spec sheet you don't need. For the rider who knows exactly what they want.

184.name: Forge
184.description: Forge is the value road bike that punches above its weight class. A butted aluminium frame, carbon fork, and Shimano Claris 2x8 groupset deliver genuine road performance at an entry-level price. Your first serious bike.

185.name: Climber R3
185.description: Climber R3 is optimised for one thing: going uphill faster than anyone around you. An ultralight carbon frame, compact gearing, and a seatpost that absorbs road buzz on the descent back down.

186.name: Storm
186.description: Storm is built for riders who ride whatever the forecast says. A tough aluminium frame, sealed bearings throughout, and a Fox 36 fork that handles mud as well as it handles dust. No excuses.

187.name: Recon
187.description: Recon is a lightweight XC hardtail built for fast singletrack and long days in the saddle. A carbon front triangle keeps the weight down while a RockShox SID fork keeps the front end planted. Race it. Explore in it. Do both.

188.name: Torque E3
188.description: Torque E3 is built for riders who demand maximum output. A Bosch Performance Line CX Race motor delivers peak power on the steepest climbs, while 160mm of travel handles whatever lies beyond them.

189.name: Wander
189.description: Wander is for the rider who sets off without a map. A versatile aluminium frame, wide 47c tyre clearance, and a dynamo hub keep you moving long after the route runs out. Follow your instincts.

190.name: Arc
190.description: Arc is a time trial-inspired aero road bike designed to cut through wind resistance. A deep-section carbon frame, integrated cockpit, and aero tube shapes deliver measurable speed gains on every flat and rolling route.

191.name: Hatch
191.description: Hatch is the practical urban e-bike for riders who want reliability above all else. A Shimano STEPS motor, Nexus 8-speed internal hub, hydraulic brakes, and full lighting as standard. Rain or shine, it gets you there.

192.name: Delta
192.description: Delta is the carbon road bike for riders ready to step up. A monocoque frame, full Shimano 105 Di2 groupset, and a wide-rim carbon wheelset. The upgrade that changes how you think about riding.

193.name: Scout
193.description: Scout is the gravel bike for curious riders who want to find out what's around the next bend. A mid-range aluminium frame, reliable mechanical disc brakes, and SRAM Apex 1x11 give you honest performance on any surface.

194.name: Ridge Runner
194.description: Ridge Runner is a do-everything trail hardtail for riders who want one bike that handles it all. Enduro-inspired geometry, a 130mm fork, and wide bars give it more trail confidence than its price tag suggests.

195.name: Metro E3 Cargo
195.description: Metro E3 Cargo is designed around one question: what do you need to carry? A reinforced frame, dual child seat mounts, and a front cargo platform make it the urban e-bike that replaces two school runs and a shopping trip.

196.name: Pinnacle
196.description: Pinnacle is the HyBikes flagship road bike. Every component chosen without compromise. A sub-750g carbon frame, full Shimano Dura-Ace Di2, lightweight ceramic bearings, and a tubeless setup that holds nothing back. This is the top of the range.

197.name: Overland
197.description: Overland is the ultimate bikepacking platform. A titanium frame that absorbs fatigue over multi-day distances, mounts everywhere you need them, and a geometry that stays confident under a full load. Built for weeks, not weekends.

198.name: Volt E2
198.description: Volt E2 is the evolution of the Volt E1. A larger 900Wh battery, a redesigned motor mount that reduces weight, and a full carbon frame that makes it the longest-range performance e-bike in the range.

199.name: Dusk
199.description: Dusk is the premium urban e-bike for riders who care about design as much as performance. A hand-welded aluminium frame with a matte finish, Enviolo automatic stepless gearing, and a Bosch motor that responds like it read your mind.

200.name: Pulse Urban
200.description: Pulse Urban is the e-bike that disappears into your routine. A sleek mid-drive motor, fully integrated battery, and a weight that makes you forget it's electric. Commute faster, arrive fresher.

201.name: Criterium SL
201.description: The Criterium SL is a road bike designed around one idea: speed through turns. Shorter chainstays, a stiffer bottom bracket, and race-day responsive handling that rewards brave riding.

202.name: Drift 650
202.description: The Drift 650 is a gravel bike that leans into the rough stuff. Oversized 650b wheels, a dropped chainstay for massive tyre clearance, and a steel frame that soaks up everything the trail throws at you.

203.name: Bolt eMTB
203.description: The Bolt eMTB puts a Shimano EP801 motor under a 170mm full-suspension platform. Climb what you couldn't before. Descend what you wouldn't before. Then do it all again — the 900Wh battery says so.
